body.title: GrannyShim: auto-start daemon when queue has dispatchable ticket
body.result: GrannyShim.start() now starts GrannyDaemon; stop() stops it; is_running() added; 3 lifecycle tests green. Criterion 5 (restart Granny) is a manual deploy step.
body.description:
GrannyShim currently has no awareness of GrannyDaemon — the daemon is started manually via tmux. This breaks the encapsulation principle: the shim should own the device's full lifecycle. When a dispatchable ticket appears in the queue (status=sprint, no gate, worker=claude/cc or CC tag), GrannyShim should auto-start the daemon if it isn't already running. This follows the same 'good design almost nobody does' pattern as SuperClaude's hidden setup — the system self-manages.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/shim.py (add daemon lifecycle awareness), devices/granny/daemon.py (expose is_running() or similar), tests/test_granny_shim.py
**Design rules:** none apply beyond existing device encapsulation rule
**Scope boundary:** IN: GrannyShim.start() also starts GrannyDaemon; add a queue-check on shim start (if dispatchable tickets exist, ensure daemon is running); GrannyShim.stop() stops daemon. OUT: IMAP-inbox-triggered start (follow-on, part of T-feeds-mcp-tools shim intelligence); tmux notification to Akien on incoming message (separate ticket). Note: also fix self_test in shim.py which still checks for 'Test plan' instead of 'Completion criteria'.
**Completion criteria:** (1) Starting GrannyShim also starts GrannyDaemon. (2) If the queue has a dispatchable CC ticket when shim starts, daemon is running within 5s. (3) Stopping GrannyShim stops the daemon cleanly. (4) tests/test_granny_shim.py covers these cases. (5) Restart checklist: restart Granny (new shim behavior takes effect).
